source_title: Learn GIS - QGIS Full Course for Beginners

concepts.name: QGIS (Quantum GIS)
concepts.definition: A free and open-source cross-platform desktop geographic information system (GIS) application that supports viewing, editing, and analysis of geospatial data.

concepts.name: Open Source Software
concepts.definition: Software with source code that anyone can inspect, modify, and enhance, often maintained by a community and free to use.

concepts.name: Plugins
concepts.definition: Extensions that can be added to the core QGIS software to provide additional functionality or tools for specific analysis tasks.

concepts.name: Spatial Analysis
concepts.definition: The process of examining the locations, attributes, and relationships of features in spatial data through overlay and other analytical techniques.

steps.explanation: To access the official download files and documentation.
steps.gotcha: Ensure you check the version number to match your documentation.

steps.explanation: This downloads a .dmg or zip file containing the application.

steps.action: Drag QGIS into the Applications folder
steps.explanation: Standard installation method for macOS software.
steps.gotcha: If an older version exists, decide whether to overwrite it or keep both.

steps.explanation: Since QGIS is not from the Mac App Store, macOS may block it initially.

gotchas.description: App cannot be opened because it is from an unidentified developer.
gotchas.context: Occurs on macOS during the first launch after installation.
gotchas.solution: Go to System Settings > Privacy & Security and select 'Open Anyway' for QGIS.
Ensure you know your specific QGIS version number (e.g., 3.34 Prizren) to find the matching documentation, as terminology and features change across versions.
